beats.activity: Waking up, shower, coffee at home in the drizzle
beats.reasoning: Starting the day at home after yesterday's late crash; yesterday's low energy (45/100) suggests a slower morning. The rain outside makes a quick indoor routine feel right.

beats.activity: Commute to tattoo studio in Fremont via local roads to avoid SR-520 closure
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound is closed 9am–noon; Liam avoids that route entirely by taking local streets from Capitol Hill to Fremont. Leaves early enough to arrive before the closure window and settle in.

beats.activity: Studio prep, client consultations, and two tattoo sessions
beats.reasoning: Steady work morning; the drizzle outside keeps foot traffic predictable. Yesterday's restless energy has settled into focus, so Liam settles into routine studio work.

beats.activity: Lunch at a local Fremont spot, staying dry indoors
beats.reasoning: Rain is still steady until 2pm; grabbing lunch within Fremont rather than venturing across town avoids exposure. A quick meal before the afternoon client rush.

beats.activity: Three tattoo sessions and client notes
beats.reasoning: Afternoon push through the rain; Liam stays focused on studio work. By 3pm the rain clears, but he's in the middle of sessions anyway.

beats.activity: Post-work walk around Fremont, grabbing a coffee and checking expense spreadsheet at a cafe
beats.reasoning: Rain has cleared by now. Liam decompresses after a solid day of work with a walk and coffee, taking time to update his meticulous expense tracking—a grounding ritual.

beats.activity: Commute from Fremont back to Capitol Hill
beats.reasoning: Heading home after a full day; no traffic constraints now that it's mid-afternoon and SR-520 closure is lifted. A straightforward cross-town drive.

beats.activity: Dinner prep and casual meal at home
beats.reasoning: Back home with time to cook a proper meal; yesterday's energy was scattered, today grounded Liam. A quiet evening ahead feels natural after an intense studio day.

beats.activity: Video gaming and winding down, some open-mic poetry browsing online
beats.reasoning: A lower-key evening at home; Liam has gaming and poetry interests, both can happen indoors. Yesterday's late-night gaming session was energizing but exhausting, so tonight is a gentler pace.

beats.activity: Reading, light stretching, preparing for bed
beats.reasoning: Final wind-down before sleep; Liam is already at home and settling in naturally after a full, balanced day.

diary:
Gray morning in Capitol Hill, coffee tasted better than usual while watching the rain streak the windows. Took the long way to the studio—everyone was dodging the bridge closure, but I had time. The drizzle kept the streets quiet.

Studio was steady. Five clients today, which isn't bad for a Tuesday. There's something meditative about repetition—the needle, the concentration, watching someone's vision become permanent on their skin. Grabbed lunch at Revel and just sat there, listening to the ambient noise of other people's conversations.

After work I wandered Fremont for a bit, got an espresso and actually looked at my expenses like I've been meaning to. Numbers are okay. Back home, made dinner, played some games to decompress. Found myself scrolling through open-mic poetry events later—don't know why, just felt like it. Ended the night stretching on the living room floor, feeling the kind of tired that's earned.
thought_process: The rain and bridge closure shaped my whole day without really stressing me—I just pivoted early, took local roads, no rush. There's something about gray Tuesdays that makes me want to focus inward. Work felt solid and unhurried; I wasn't running behind anyone else's schedule. The expense check happened almost accidentally at the cafe, which is good—been avoiding it. By evening I was genuinely tired but restless, so gaming helped. The poetry browsing surprised me; maybe the rain and the steady day made me sentimental or reflective. I'm settling into a rhythm lately, less manic about chasing bigger and bigger projects, more content with the craft itself and small moments like coffee at a good cafe.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
